narrative:
On July 15, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 40.8%, resolving 1,076 out of 2,635 scheduled cases across 41 judges. This represents a decline in clearance capacity compared to the previous day (July 14), which recorded a 44.1% hearing rate with 1,109 cases heard out of 2,512 scheduled. This downturn in efficiency continues a downward trend from July 13, which saw a 42.8% conversion rate, indicating that the court is struggling to keep pace as scheduled caseloads expand.

Judge-wise performance was led by Justice S Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19, who managed the day's heaviest individual workload by hearing 103 of 234 scheduled cases (44.0%). High-efficiency disposal was observed under Justice V Srishananda in Hall 21, who successfully heard 70 of 96 scheduled matters (72.9%). In contrast, Justice B M Shyam Prasad in Hall 6 had a heavily fragmented docket; while hearing 19 of 28 cases (67.9%) in one session, his largest scheduled blocks of 125 and 79 cases concluded with zero hearings.

From a procedural stage perspective, cases listed for "Orders" made up the largest volume at 855 scheduled matters, converting at a 40.0% rate. This reflects a drop from the 51.0% conversion rate for Orders on July 14. "Admission" matters saw the strongest momentum, converting 268 of 515 scheduled cases (52.0%), while "Final Hearings" remained backlogged, with only 21.7% of the 456 scheduled cases being heard. 

Case type distribution was heavily concentrated in Writ Petitions (WP), which comprised 1,234 of the scheduled cases but achieved a poor conversion rate of 33.0%—a significant drop from the 41.0% conversion rate for WPs on the previous day. Conversely, Regular Second Appeals (RSA) achieved a higher hearing rate of 58.7% (88 heard of 150), and Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) converted 47.1% of their 323 scheduled cases.
